{"path":"E:\硬件开发\project_jun08a_new\simulator.py","replaceMode":"whole","content":"#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Claude Code Status Monitor - Hardware Simulator
================================================
Preview the status monitor without physical hardware.
Simulates TFT display, buzzer sounds, solenoid tapping, and button.
Matches the ESP32 firmware animations pixel-for-pixel (scaled 2x).

Run with: python simulator.py
Or via bridge: python bridge.py --mode simulate
"""

import tkinter as tk
from tkinter import ttk
import threading
import time
import math
import sys
import os

# Try to import winsound for Windows beep simulation
try:
    import winsound
    HAS_WINSOUND = True
except ImportError:
    HAS_WINSOUND = False

# Cat color (tft.color565(255, 120, 50) ≈ #FF7832)
CAT_COLOR = '#FF7832'
EYE_WHITE = '#FFFFFF'
EYE_BLACK = '#000000'
HAT_COLOR = '#FF3232'

# ST77XX color equivalents
ST77XX_BLACK = '#000000'
ST77XX_WHITE = '#FFFFFF'
ST77XX_RED = '#FF0000'
ST77XX_GREEN = '#00FF00'
ST77XX_BLUE = '#0000FF'
ST77XX_CYAN = '#00FFFF'
ST77XX_MAGENTA = '#FF00FF'
ST77XX_YELLOW = '#FFFF00'
ST77XX_ORANGE = '#FF7832'

# Scale factor (ESP32 160x80 -> display 320x160)
SCALE = 2

def scale_xy(x, y):
    return x * SCALE, y * SCALE

def scale_wh(w, h):
    return w * SCALE, h * SCALE


class HardwareSimulator:
    """Simulates ESP32 hardware: TFT, buzzer, solenoid, button"""

    def __init__(self):
        self.root = tk.Tk()
        self.root.title("Claude Monitor - Hardware Simulator")
        self.root.configure(bg='#1a1a1a')
        self.root.resizable(False, False)

        # State variables
        self.current_state = "idle"
        self.anim_frame = 0
        self.muted = False
        self.solenoid_active = False

        # Animation timers
        self.anim_running = True
        self.last_anim_time = 0
        self.state_start_time = 0

        # Button
        self.button_press_time = 0

        # Create GUI
        self._create_gui()

        # Start animation thread
        self.anim_thread = threading.Thread(target=self._animation_loop, daemon=True)
        self.anim_thread.start()

    # ─── Drawing Helpers (matching ESP32 tft.fillRect etc.) ───

    def _fill_rect(self, x, y, w, h, color):
        """Simulate tft.fillRect()"""
        sx, sy = scale_xy(x, y)
        sw, sh = scale_wh(w, h)
        self.tft_canvas.create_rectangle(sx, sy, sx + sw, sy + sh, fill=color, outline='')

    def _draw_rect(self, x, y, w, h, color, width=1):
        """Simulate tft.drawRect()"""
        sx, sy = scale_xy(x, y)
        sw, sh = scale_wh(w, h)
        self.tft_canvas.create_rectangle(sx, sy, sx + sw, sy + sh, outline=color, width=width * SCALE)

    def _fill_circle(self, cx, cy, r, color):
        """Simulate tft.fillCircle()"""
        sx, sy = scale_xy(cx, cy)
        sr = r * SCALE
        self.tft_canvas.create_oval(sx - sr, sy - sr, sx + sr, sy + sr, fill=color, outline='')

    def _draw_circle(self, cx, cy, r, color, width=1):
        """Simulate tft.drawCircle()"""
        sx, sy = scale_xy(cx, cy)
        sr = r * SCALE
        self.tft_canvas.create_oval(sx - sr, sy - sr, sx + sr, sy + sr, outline=color, width=width * SCALE)

    def _draw_text(self, x, y, text, color, size=1):
        """Simulate tft.print() at cursor position"""
        sx, sy = scale_xy(x, y)
        font_size = size * 8 * SCALE
        font = ('Courier', font_size, 'bold') if size >= 2 else ('Courier', font_size)
        self.tft_canvas.create_text(sx, sy, text=text, fill=color, font=font, anchor='nw')

    def _fill_screen(self, color):
        """Simulate tft.fillScreen()"""
        self.tft_canvas.create_rectangle(0, 0, 320, 160, fill=color, outline='')

    # ─── Cat Character Drawing ───

    def _draw_cat(self, bx, by, eye_h=5, pupil=True, mouth=False, arm_offset=0):
        """
        Draw the cat character at (bx, by).
        Matches ESP32 pixel-art cat exactly.
        body: 40x24, ears: 8x6 and 8x6, eyes: 6xeye_h, legs: 4x 6x4
        """
        col = CAT_COLOR

        # Body
        self._fill_rect(bx, by, 40, 24, col)
        # Left ear
        self._fill_rect(bx - 8, by + 4, 8, 6, col)
        # Right ear
        self._fill_rect(bx + 40, by + 4, 8, 6, col)
        # Left eye white
        self._fill_rect(bx + 8, by + 2, 6, eye_h, EYE_WHITE)
        # Right eye white
        self._fill_rect(bx + 26, by + 2, 6, eye_h, EYE_WHITE)

        if pupil and eye_h >= 3:
            # Left pupil
            self._fill_rect(bx + 10, by + 3, 3, 3, EYE_BLACK)
            # Right pupil
            self._fill_rect(bx + 28, by + 3, 3, 3, EYE_BLACK)

        if mouth:
            # Open mouth (for permission state)
            self._fill_rect(bx + 12, by + 14, 16, 4, EYE_BLACK)

        # Legs (4 legs)
        self._fill_rect(bx + 2, by + 24, 6, 4, col)
        self._fill_rect(bx + 12, by + 24, 6, 4, col)
        self._fill_rect(bx + 22, by + 24, 6, 4, col)
        self._fill_rect(bx + 32, by + 24, 6, 4, col)

        # Arm (for coding state)
        if arm_offset != 0:
            self._fill_rect(bx + 40, by + 10 + arm_offset, 8, 4, col)

    # ─── State Animations (matching ESP32 firmware) ───

    def _draw_idle(self):
        """Idle: cat sleeping with Zzz bubbles, breathing animation"""
        f = self.anim_frame % 8

        # Breathing: slight y offset
        by = 35 + (2 if f % 2 == 1 else 0)
        bx = 60

        self._draw_cat(bx, by, eye_h=3, pupil=False)

        # Zzz bubbles (frames 0-3 show Z, z, z)
        if f < 4:
            zy = 15 - f * 4
            self._draw_text(bx + 50, zy, "Z", ST77XX_CYAN, 1)
            if f >= 1:
                self._draw_text(bx + 58, zy + 8, "z", ST77XX_CYAN, 1)
            if f >= 2:
                self._draw_text(bx + 64, zy + 14, "z", ST77XX_CYAN, 1)

        # Status text
        self._draw_text(5, 65, "sleeping...", ST77XX_CYAN, 1)

        # Beep
        if f == 0 and not self.muted:
            self._play_beep(330, 40)

    def _draw_thinking(self):
        """Thinking: cat with thought bubbles and dots"""
        f = self.anim_frame % 12
        bx, by = 60, 30

        # Blinking eyes
        eye_h = 3 if f < 6 else 4
        self._draw_cat(bx, by, eye_h=eye_h, pupil=(eye_h == 3))

        # Thought bubbles
        bub = f % 3
        self._draw_circle(bx + 55, by - 5, 8 + bub * 4, ST77XX_YELLOW)
        self._draw_circle(bx + 55, by - 5, 4 + bub * 2, ST77XX_YELLOW)

        # Dots animation
        dots = f % 4
        dot_text = "." * (dots + 1)
        self._draw_text(bx + 48, by - 12, dot_text, ST77XX_YELLOW, 2)

        # Status text
        self._draw_text(5, 65, "thinking...", ST77XX_YELLOW, 1)

        # Beep
        if not self.muted:
            self._play_beep(500 + f * 60, 25)

    def _draw_coding(self):
        """Coding: terminal window with scrolling code + cat typing"""
        f = self.anim_frame % 20
        bx, by = 10, 5

        # Terminal window
        term_color = '#006400'  # dark green border
        self._draw_rect(bx, by, 70, 50, term_color)
        self._fill_rect(bx + 1, by + 1, 68, 48, ST77XX_BLACK)

        # Scrolling code lines
        code_lines = ["void ", "int x ", "if(x)", "for(", "  x++", "}", "return", "delay", "Serial", "tone"]
        for i in range(6):
            line_idx = (f // 2 + i) % 10
            ly = by + 3 + i * 8
            prefix = ">" if line_idx % 3 == 0 else " "
            self._draw_text(bx + 3, ly, prefix + " " + code_lines[line_idx], ST77XX_GREEN, 1)

        # Cat (to the right, typing)
        cx = 95
        cy = 25 + (2 if f % 2 == 1 else 0)
        arm_off = 3 if f % 2 == 1 else 0
        self._draw_cat(cx, cy, eye_h=4, pupil=True, arm_offset=arm_off)

        # Status text
        self._draw_text(95, 60, "coding...", ST77XX_GREEN, 1)

        # Beep
        if f == 0 and not self.muted:
            self._play_beep(1000, 15)

    def _draw_done(self):
        """Done: cat jumping with party hat, confetti, OK!"""
        f = self.anim_frame % 10
        bx = 55

        # Jump animation
        jump_y = 0
        if f < 4:
            jump_y = -((4 - f) * 5)
        by = 30 + jump_y

        # Eyes: wide open when jumping, normal after landing
        eye_h = 5 if f < 5 else 3
        self._draw_cat(bx, by, eye_h=eye_h, pupil=(eye_h == 3))

        # Smile (after landing)
        if eye_h == 3:
            self._fill_rect(bx + 14, by + 12, 12, 3, EYE_BLACK)

        # Party hat (red triangle)
        self._fill_rect(bx + 18, by - 10, 4, 10, HAT_COLOR)
        self._fill_circle(bx + 20, by - 12, 5, HAT_COLOR)

        # Confetti
        confetti_colors = [ST77XX_RED, ST77XX_YELLOW, ST77XX_GREEN, ST77XX_CYAN, ST77XX_MAGENTA]
        for i in range(8):
            cx = 10 + (i * 19 + f * 7) % 150
            cy = 5 + (i * 13 + f * 5) % 70
            self._fill_rect(cx, cy, 3, 3, confetti_colors[i % 5])

        # OK! text
        self._draw_text(100, 30, "OK!", ST77XX_GREEN, 2)
        self._draw_text(5, 65, "done! ^_^", ST77XX_GREEN, 1)

        # Beep
        if not self.muted:
            self._play_beep(1200 + f * 50, 60)

        # Solenoid on state change
        if f == 0:
            self._activate_solenoid(300)

    def _draw_permission(self):
        """Permission: red/black flashing, cat with question mark, PRESS BTN"""
        f = self.anim_frame % 2

        # Flashing background
        bg = '#3C0000' if f else ST77XX_BLACK
        self._fill_screen(bg)

        bx, by = 55, 25

        # Cat with wide eyes and open mouth
        self._draw_cat(bx, by, eye_h=5, pupil=True, mouth=True)

        # Question mark dialog box
        qx, qy = bx - 20, by - 15
        self._draw_rect(qx, qy, 30, 22, ST77XX_YELLOW)
        self._fill_rect(qx + 1, qy + 1, 28, 20, ST77XX_BLACK)
        self._draw_text(qx + 6, qy + 2, "?", ST77XX_YELLOW, 2)

        # Flashing PRESS BTN text
        text_color = ST77XX_RED if f else ST77XX_YELLOW
        self._draw_text(5, 65, "[PRESS BTN]", text_color, 1)

        # Beep
        if not self.muted:
            self._play_beep(1800 if f else 600, 80)

        # Solenoid
        if f:
            self._activate_solenoid(150)

    # ─── Sound & Solenoid ───

    def _play_beep(self, freq, duration):
        if self.muted or not HAS_WINSOUND:
            return
        try:
            threading.Thread(target=lambda: winsound.Beep(freq, duration), daemon=True).start()
        except:
            pass
        self._flash_buzzer()

    def _flash_buzzer(self):
        self.buzzer_indicator.itemconfig('buzzer', fill='#00ff00')
        self.root.after(100, lambda: self.buzzer_indicator.itemconfig('buzzer', fill='gray'))

    def _activate_solenoid(self, duration_ms):
        if self.solenoid_active:
            return
        self.solenoid_active = True
        self.solenoid_indicator.itemconfig('solenoid', fill='#ff6600')
        self.solenoid_label.config(text="ON ⚡")
        if not self.muted and HAS_WINSOUND:
            try:
                threading.Thread(target=lambda: winsound.Beep(100, min(duration_ms, 200)), daemon=True).start()
            except:
                pass
        self.root.after(duration_ms, self._deactivate_solenoid)

    def _deactivate_solenoid(self):
        self.solenoid_active = False
        self.solenoid_indicator.itemconfig('solenoid', fill='gray')
        self.solenoid_label.config(text="OFF")

    # ─── Button ───

    def _button_press(self, event):
        self.button_canvas.itemconfig('button', fill='#00ff00')
        self.button_press_time = time.time()

    def _button_release(self, event):
        self.button_canvas.itemconfig('button', fill='#444')
        held = time.time() - self.button_press_time

        if held > 0.7:
            # Long press = mute toggle
            self.muted = not self.muted
            self.mute_var.set(self.muted)
            self.button_log.config(text="🔇 Muted" if self.muted else "🔊 Unmuted")
            if not self.muted:
                self._play_beep(800, 100)
        else:
            # Short press = confirm
            self.button_log.config(text="✓ Confirmed!")
            if not self.muted:
                self._play_beep(1000, 50)
            if self.current_state == "permission":
                self._set_state("idle")

    # ─── State Control ───

    def _set_state(self, state):
        self.current_state = state
        self.anim_frame = 0
        self.state_start_time = time.time()

        state_icons = {
            "idle": "💤 Idle",
            "thinking": "🤔 Thinking",
            "coding": "💻 Coding",
            "done": "✅ Done",
            "permission": "⚠️ Permission"
        }
        self.status_var.set(state_icons.get(state, state))

        if state == "done":
            self._activate_solenoid(300)
        elif state == "permission":
            self._activate_solenoid(200)

    def _toggle_mute(self):
        self.muted = self.mute_var.get()
        self.button_log.config(text="🔇 Muted" if self.muted else "🔊 Unmuted")
        if not self.muted:
            self._play_beep(800, 100)

    # ─── Animation Loop ───

    def _animation_loop(self):
        """Background animation loop matching ESP32 frame rates"""
        while self.anim_running:
            self.root.after(0, self._draw_tft)
            # Match ESP32 frame delays per state
            if self.current_state == "idle":
                time.sleep(0.5)
            elif self.current_state == "thinking":
                time.sleep(0.35)
            elif self.current_state == "coding":
                time.sleep(0.2)
            elif self.current_state == "done":
                time.sleep(0.3)
            elif self.current_state == "permission":
                time.sleep(0.4)
            else:
                time.sleep(0.05)

    def _draw_tft(self):
        """Main draw callback"""
        self.tft_canvas.delete('all')

        # Default black background (permission handles its own)
        if self.current_state != "permission":
            self._fill_screen(ST77XX_BLACK)

        # Draw current state
        if self.current_state == "idle":
            self._draw_idle()
        elif self.current_state == "thinking":
            self._draw_thinking()
        elif self.current_state == "coding":
            self._draw_coding()
        elif self.current_state == "done":
            self._draw_done()
        elif self.current_state == "permission":
            self._draw_permission()

        self.anim_frame += 1

    # ─── GUI Setup ───

    def _create_gui(self):
        main_frame = ttk.Frame(self.root, padding=10)
        main_frame.pack(fill=tk.BOTH, expand=True)

        style = ttk.Style()
        style.configure('TFrame', background='#1a1a1a')
        style.configure('TLabel', background='#1a1a1a', foreground='white')
        style.configure('TLabelframe', background='#1a1a1a', foreground='white')
        style.configure('TLabelframe.Label', background='#1a1a1a', foreground='white')

        # === TFT Display (160x80 scaled 2x) ===
        tft_frame = ttk.LabelFrame(main_frame, text="📺 TFT Display (0.96\" 160×80 ×2)", padding=5)
        tft_frame.pack(pady=5)

        self.tft_canvas = tk.Canvas(
            tft_frame, width=160 * SCALE, height=80 * SCALE,
            bg='black', highlightthickness=2, highlightbackground='#333'
        )
        self.tft_canvas.pack()

        # === Status ===
        status_frame = ttk.Frame(main_frame)
        status_frame.pack(fill=tk.X, pady=5)

        ttk.Label(status_frame, text="Current State:", font=('Arial', 10)).pack(side=tk.LEFT)
        self.status_var = tk.StringVar(value="💤 idle")
        self.status_label = ttk.Label(
            status_frame, textvariable=self.status_var,
            font=('Arial', 12, 'bold'), foreground='#00ff88'
        )
        self.status_label.pack(side=tk.LEFT, padx=10)

        # === Hardware Indicators ===
        hw_frame = ttk.Frame(main_frame)
        hw_frame.pack(fill=tk.X, pady=5)

        # Buzzer
        buzzer_frame = ttk.LabelFrame(hw_frame, text="🔊 Buzzer", padding=3)
        buzzer_frame.pack(side=tk.LEFT, padx=5, fill=tk.Y)
        self.buzzer_indicator = tk.Canvas(buzzer_frame, width=24, height=24, bg='#1a1a1a', highlightthickness=0)
        self.buzzer_indicator.pack()
        self.buzzer_indicator.create_oval(2, 2, 22, 22, fill='gray', outline='#666', tags='buzzer')

        # Solenoid
        solenoid_frame = ttk.LabelFrame(hw_frame, text="⚡ Solenoid", padding=3)
        solenoid_frame.pack(side=tk.LEFT, padx=5, fill=tk.Y)
        self.solenoid_indicator = tk.Canvas(solenoid_frame, width=50, height=24, bg='#1a1a1a', highlightthickness=0)
        self.solenoid_indicator.pack()
        self.solenoid_indicator.create_rectangle(2, 2, 48, 22, fill='gray', outline='#666', tags='solenoid')
        self.solenoid_label = ttk.Label(solenoid_frame, text="OFF", font=('Arial', 9))
        self.solenoid_label.pack()

        # Mute
        mute_frame = ttk.Frame(hw_frame)
        mute_frame.pack(side=tk.LEFT, padx=10)
        self.mute_var = tk.BooleanVar(value=False)
        ttk.Checkbutton(mute_frame, text="🔇 Mute", variable=self.mute_var, command=self._toggle_mute).pack()

        # Button
        btn_frame = ttk.LabelFrame(hw_frame, text="🔘 Button", padding=3)
        btn_frame.pack(side=tk.LEFT, padx=5, fill=tk.Y)
        self.button_canvas = tk.Canvas(btn_frame, width=50, height=30, bg='#1a1a1a', highlightthickness=0)
        self.button_canvas.pack()
        self.button_canvas.create_rectangle(10, 5, 40, 25, fill='#444', outline='#888', tags='button')
        self.button_canvas.bind('<Button-1>', self._button_press)
        self.button_canvas.bind('<ButtonRelease-1>', self._button_release)
        self.button_log = ttk.Label(btn_frame, text="click me", font=('Arial', 8))
        self.button_log.pack()

        # === State Control ===
        control_frame = ttk.LabelFrame(main_frame, text="🎮 Manual State Control", padding=5)
        control_frame.pack(fill=tk.X, pady=5)

        states = [
            ("💤 Idle", "idle", '#00ffff'),
            ("🤔 Thinking", "thinking", '#ffff00'),
            ("💻 Coding", "coding", '#00ff00'),
            ("✅ Done", "done", '#00ff00'),
            ("⚠️ Permission", "permission", '#ff4444')
        ]

        for text, state, color in states:
            tk.Button(
                control_frame, text=text,
                command=lambda s=state: self._set_state(s),
                bg='#333', fg=color, font=('Arial', 10), width=13,
                activebackground='#555', activeforeground=color,
                relief=tk.RAISED, bd=2
            ).pack(side=tk.LEFT, padx=2)

        # === Auto Demo + Quit ===
        bottom_frame = ttk.Frame(main_frame)
        bottom_frame.pack(fill=tk.X, pady=8)

        self.demo_var = tk.BooleanVar(value=False)
        self.demo_btn = ttk.Checkbutton(bottom_frame, text="🔄 Auto Demo", variable=self.demo_var, command=self._toggle_demo)
        self.demo_btn.pack(side=tk.LEFT)

        ttk.Button(bottom_frame, text="❌ Quit", command=self._quit).pack(side=tk.RIGHT)

        ttk.Label(
            main_frame,
            text="Pixel-perfect simulation of ESP32 firmware animations.\nNo hardware needed! Short press = confirm, Long press = mute toggle.",
            font=('Arial', 9), foreground='#888'
        ).pack(pady=3)

    def _toggle_demo(self):
        if self.demo_var.get():
            self._demo_loop()

    def _demo_loop(self):
        if not self.demo_var.get() or not self.anim_running:
            return
        demo_seq = [
            ("idle", 3),
            ("thinking", 4),
            ("coding", 5),
            ("done", 3),
            ("idle", 2),
            ("permission", 4),
            ("coding", 4),
            ("done", 3),
        ]
        for state, duration in demo_seq:
            if not self.demo_var.get() or not self.anim_running:
                return
            self._set_state(state)
            time.sleep(duration)
        # Loop
        if self.demo_var.get() and self.anim_running:
            self.root.after(100, self._demo_loop)

    def _quit(self):
        self.anim_running = False
        self.root.quit()
        self.root.destroy()

    def run(self):
        self.root.update_idletasks()
        w = self.root.winfo_width()
        h = self.root.winfo_height()
        x = (self.root.winfo_screenwidth() // 2) - (w // 2)
        y = (self.root.winfo_screenheight() // 2) - (h // 2)
        self.root.geometry(f'+{x}+{y}')
        self.root.mainloop()


def run_simulator():
    sim = HardwareSimulator()
    sim.run()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    run_simulator()
"}

# rullerzhou-afk/clawd-on-desk A pixel desktop pet that watches Claude Code, Codex, Cursor & other AI coding agents — so you don't have to. **语言:** JavaScript **Stars:** 3925 | **Forks:** 377 **开源协议:** AGPL-3.0 **最后更新:** 2026-06-08T06:34:06Z **默认分支:** main **主页:** 无 **仓库地址:** https://github.com/rullerzhou-afk/clawd-on-desk --- ## README

Clawd on Desk

中文版 · 繁體中文 · 한국어 · 日本語

🌏 Don't see your language? Open a PR to add one — Español, Français, Deutsch, etc. all welcome.

Clawd lives on your desktop and reacts to what your AI coding agent is doing — in real time. Start a long task, walk away, come back when the crab tells you it's done. Thinking when you prompt, typing when tools run, grooving or juggling for subagents, reviewing permissions, celebrating when tasks complete, sleeping when you step away. Ships with three built-in themes: **Clawd** (pixel crab), **Calico** (三花猫), and **Cloudling** (云宝), with full support for custom themes and imported Codex Pet animation packs. > Supports Windows 11, macOS, and Ubuntu/Linux. Windows releases provide separate x64 and ARM64 installers. Source builds require Node.js. Works with **Claude Code**, **Codex CLI**, **Copilot CLI**, **Gemini CLI**, **Antigravity CLI (agy)**, **Cursor Agent**, **CodeBuddy**, **Kiro CLI**, **Kimi Code CLI (Kimi-CLI)**, **Qwen Code**, **opencode**, **Pi**, **OpenClaw**, and **Hermes Agent**. ## Features ### Multi-Agent Support - **Claude Code** — full integration via command hooks + HTTP permission hooks - **Codex CLI** — official hooks with JSONL fallback (`~/.codex/sessions/`), registered automatically with real permission bubbles - **Copilot CLI** — command hooks via `~/.copilot/hooks/hooks.json` - **Gemini CLI** — command hooks via `~/.gemini/settings.json` (registered automatically when Clawd starts, or run `npm run install:gemini-hooks`) - **Antigravity CLI (agy)** — command hooks via `~/.gemini/config/hooks.json` (registered automatically when Antigravity config exists, or run `npm run install:antigravity-hooks`); **state-only**: Clawd never pops a permission bubble for agy. Every Allow / Deny / Always-allow choice happens in agy's own terminal menu - **Cursor Agent** — [Cursor IDE hooks](https://cursor.com/docs/agent/hooks) in `~/.cursor/hooks.json` (registered automatically when Clawd starts, or run `npm run install:cursor-hooks`) - **CodeBuddy** — Claude Code-compatible command hooks + HTTP permission hooks via `~/.codebuddy/settings.json` (registered automatically when Clawd starts, or run `node hooks/codebuddy-install.js`) - **Kiro CLI** — command hooks injected into custom agent configs under `~/.kiro/agents/`, plus an auto-created `clawd` agent that is re-synced from Kiro's built-in `kiro_default` whenever Clawd starts, so you can opt into hooks with minimal behavior drift via `kiro-cli --agent clawd` or `/agent swap clawd` (registered automatically when Clawd starts, or run `npm run install:kiro-hooks`). State hooks are verified on macOS and Windows. - **Kimi Code CLI (Kimi-CLI)** — command hooks via `~/.kimi/config.toml` (`[[hooks]]` entries) (registered automatically when Clawd starts, or run `npm run install:kimi-hooks`) - **Qwen Code** — command hooks via `~/.qwen/settings.json` (registered automatically when Clawd starts, or run `npm run install:qwen-hooks`); state tracking and Qwen `PermissionRequest` desktop approval bubbles are supported - **opencode** — [plugin integration](https://opencode.ai/docs/plugins) via `~/.config/opencode/opencode.json` (registered automatically when Clawd starts); zero-latency event streaming, permission bubbles with Allow/Always/Deny, and building animations when parallel subagents are spawned via the `task` tool - **Pi** — global extension via `~/.pi/agent/extensions/clawd-on-desk` (registered automatically when Clawd starts, or run `npm run install:pi-extension`); state-only interactive lifecycle and tool activity updates while preserving Pi's default YOLO behavior - **OpenClaw** — state-only plugin integration via `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` (registered automatically when an OpenClaw config already exists, or run `npm run install:openclaw-plugin`); local `openclaw tui --local` sessions drive Clawd animations, without permission bubbles or terminal focus in Phase 1 - **Hermes Agent** — [plugin integration](https://hermes-agent.org/) via Hermes' managed plugin directory (registered automatically when Hermes is installed, or run `npm run install:hermes-plugin`); state, sessions, SessionEnd, and terminal focus are supported - **Multi-agent coexistence** — run all agents simultaneously; Clawd tracks each session independently ### Animations & Interaction - **Real-time state awareness** — agent hooks and log polling drive Clawd's animations automatically - **12 animated states** — idle, thinking, typing, building, subagent groove, multi-subagent juggling, error, happy, notification, sweeping, carrying, sleeping - **Codex Pet imports** — import Codex Pet zip packages from `Settings…` → `Theme`; Clawd adapts their atlas animations into managed themes - **Eye tracking** — Clawd follows your cursor in idle state, with body lean and shadow stretch - **Sleep sequence** — yawning, dozing, collapsing, sleeping after 60s idle; mouse movement triggers a startled wake-up animation - **Click reactions** — double-click for a poke, 4 clicks for a flail - **Drag from any state** — grab Clawd anytime (Pointer Capture prevents fast-flick drops), release to resume - **Mini mode** — drag to right edge or right-click "Mini Mode"; Clawd hides at screen edge with peek-on-hover, mini alerts/celebrations, and parabolic jump transitions ### Permission Bubble - **In-app permission review** — when Claude Code, Codex CLI, CodeBuddy, or opencode request supported tool permissions, Clawd pops a floating bubble card instead of waiting in the terminal - **Allow / deny / agent-native extras** — one-click approve or reject, plus permission rules / `Always` actions when the source agent supports them - **Global hotkeys** — `Ctrl+Shift+Y` to Allow, `Ctrl+Shift+N` to Deny the latest permission bubble (only registered while bubbles are visible) - **Stacking layout** — multiple permission requests stack upward from the bottom-right corner - **Auto-dismiss** — if you answer in the terminal first, the bubble disappears automatically - **Per-agent toggle** — open `Settings…` → `Agents`, pick an agent, and turn off `Show pop-up bubbles` to keep prompts in that agent's own terminal/TUI ### Session Intelligence - **Multi-session tracking** — sessions across all agents resolve to the highest-priority state - **Subagent awareness** — headphones groove for 1 subagent, three-ball juggling for 2+ - **Sessions dashboard + HUD** — right-click or tray → `Open Dashboard` to inspect live sessions, recent events, aliases, and jump to a terminal; a compact HUD near Clawd keeps current live sessions visible - **Terminal focus** — Dashboard/HUD actions jump to a specific session's terminal window; notification/attention states auto-focus the relevant terminal - **Process liveness detection** — detects crashed/exited supported agent processes and cleans up orphan sessions - **Startup recovery** — if Clawd restarts while any supported agent is still running, it stays awake instead of falling asleep ### System - **Click-through** — transparent areas pass clicks to windows below; only Clawd's body is interactive - **Position memory** — Clawd remembers where you left it across restarts (including mini mode) - **Single instance lock** — prevents duplicate Clawd windows - **Auto-start** — Claude Code's SessionStart hook can launch Clawd automatically if it's not running - **Do Not Disturb** — right-click or tray menu to enter sleep mode; all hook events are silenced until you wake Clawd. Permission bubbles are suppressed during DND — Codex and opencode fall back to their native prompts, while Claude Code and CodeBuddy fall back to their built-in permission flow. Antigravity and Pi are state-only integrations. - **Sound effects** — short audio cues on task completion and permission requests (toggle via right-click menu; 10s cooldown, auto-muted during DND) - **System tray** — resize (S/M/L), DND mode, language switch, auto-start, check for updates - **i18n** — English, Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese, Korean, and Japanese UI; switch via right-click menu or tray - **Auto-update** — checks GitHub releases; Windows installs NSIS updates on quit, macOS/Linux `git pull` + restart when running from a cloned repo ## Animations

Idle
Thought Bubble
Typing
Building
1 Subagent
2+ Subagents

Calico Idle
Calico Thinking
Calico Typing
Calico Building
Calico Juggling
Calico Conducting

Cloudling Idle
Cloudling Thinking
Cloudling Typing
Cloudling Building
Cloudling Juggling
Cloudling Conducting
Full event-to-state mapping, mini mode, and click reactions: **[docs/guides/state-mapping.md](docs/guides/state-mapping.md)** ## Multi-display Clawd adapts to multi-monitor setups: proportional sizing uses the display Clawd launches on, portrait monitors get a bounded boost so the pet stays readable on tall narrow screens, and you can drag Clawd across displays.

Want to see the real multi-monitor behavior? Watch the demo video in this repository.

## Quick Start For normal use, download the latest prebuilt installer from **[GitHub Releases](https://github.com/rullerzhou-afk/clawd-on-desk/releases/latest)**: - **Windows**: `Clawd-on-Desk-Setup--x64.exe` or `Clawd-on-Desk-Setup--arm64.exe` - **macOS**: `.dmg` - **Linux**: `.AppImage` or `.deb` Launch Clawd after installing it; supported agent hooks/plugins are synced automatically on startup. Run from source only if you're contributing, testing unreleased code, or debugging integrations. Source installs download Electron/build tooling and can create a large `node_modules` tree. ```bash # Clone the repo git clone https://github.com/rullerzhou-afk/clawd-on-desk.git cd clawd-on-desk # Install dependencies npm install # Start Clawd (auto-registers Claude Code hooks on launch) npm start ``` **Claude Code**, **Codex CLI**, and **Copilot CLI** work out of the box with auto-registered hooks. **Gemini CLI**, **Antigravity CLI (agy)**, **Cursor Agent**, **CodeBuddy**, **Kiro CLI**, **Kimi Code CLI (Kimi-CLI)**, **Qwen Code**, **opencode**, **Pi**, **OpenClaw**, and **Hermes Agent** auto-register when Clawd launches (if they're installed; OpenClaw also needs an initialized config). Also covers remote SSH, WSL, and platform-specific notes (macOS / Linux): **[docs/guides/setup-guide.md](docs/guides/setup-guide.md)** Want to run Claude Code / Codex CLI on a remote server and surface state plus permission bubbles in your local Clawd? Use the in-app **Settings → Remote SSH → One-click deploy**. Full walkthrough, Doctor boundary, and FAQ: **[docs/guides/guide-remote-ssh.md](docs/guides/guide-remote-ssh.md)** For the official `Codex + WSL` status, Clawd's current implementation boundary, and why this is easy to misread, see: **[docs/guides/codex-wsl-clarification.md](docs/guides/codex-wsl-clarification.md)** ## Known Limitations Some agents have feature gaps (no permission bubble, polling latency, no terminal focus). See the full table: **[docs/guides/known-limitations.md](docs/guides/known-limitations.md)** ## Custom Themes Clawd supports custom themes — replace the default crab with your own character and animations. If you already have a Codex Pet package, import its zip from `Settings…` → `Theme` → `Import pet zip`; Clawd turns the atlas into a managed theme automatically. **Quick start:** 1. Scaffold a theme: ```bash node scripts/create-theme.js my-theme # or npm run create-theme -- my-theme ``` No argument also works: it creates the next available `my-theme` scaffold in your user themes directory. 2. Edit `theme.json` and create your assets (SVG, GIF, APNG, WebP, PNG, JPG, or JPEG) 3. Restart Clawd or open `Settings…` → `Theme` → select your theme **Minimum viable theme:** 1 SVG (idle with eye tracking) + 7 GIF/APNG files (thinking, working, error, happy, notification, sleeping, waking). Eye tracking can be disabled to use any format for all states. Validate your theme before distributing: ```bash node scripts/validate-theme.js path/to/your-theme ``` Theme cards in `Settings…` → `Theme` now expose capability badges such as `Tracked idle`, `Static theme`, `Mini`, `Direct sleep`, and `No reactions`, so users can tell what a theme supports before switching. See [docs/guides/guide-theme-creation.md](docs/guides/guide-theme-creation.md) for the full creation guide with tiered paths (beginner → advanced), `theme.json` field reference, and asset guidelines. > Third-party SVG files are automatically sanitized for security. ### Roadmap Some things we'd like to explore in the future: - Codex terminal focus via process tree lookup from `codex.exe` PID - Theme registry and in-app download - Hook uninstall script for clean app removal ## Contributing Clawd on Desk is a community-driven project. Bug reports, feature ideas, and pull requests are all welcome — open an [issue](https://github.com/rullerzhou-afk/clawd-on-desk/issues) to discuss or submit a PR directly. ### Maintainers

response.content:
<think>The user asked me to update the animations to match the Clawd pixel crab from the clawd-on-desk project. I've:

1. Updated the ESP32 firmware (project.abs) - compiled successfully ✅
2. Updated the simulator.py - written successfully ✅

Let me summarize what was done.</think>已完成全部更新，ESP32 固件编译通过 ✅，simulator.py 同步更新 ✅

### 🦀 Clawd 像素螃蟹动画更新

| 状态 | 动画效果 | 对应原项目 |
|------|---------|-----------|
| 💤 Idle | 螃蟹站立，偶尔眨眼，腿交替动，偶尔冒蓝色气泡 | clawd-idle |
| 🤔 Thinking | 螃蟹静止，头顶黄色思考气泡逐帧放大 + 动态点点 | clawd-thinking |
| 💻 Typing | 螃蟹双钳交替敲击（左右摆动）+ 左侧绿色终端窗口滚动代码 | clawd-typing |
| ✅ Done | 螃蟹跳跃（抛物线），大眼，钳子举起，彩色纸屑飘落 + OK! | clawd-happy |
| ⚠️ Permission | 螃蟹大眼睛，红黑背景闪烁，头顶黄色问号对话框 + [PRESS BTN] | clawd-bubble |

**螃蟹像素构成**（橙红色壳 `#FF6432` + 浅色腹部 `#FF9650` + 深色钳尖 `#C85028`）：
- 椭圆壳体 16×12 + 高光腹部 12×8
- 双眼（白底+黑瞳），支持 normal/blink/wide 三种表情
- 左右钳（深色指尖），支持动画偏移
- 4 对腿，支持交替步态

运行 `python simulator.py` 即可预览效果。

toolCalls.name: edit_file

{"path": "E:\硬件开发\project_jun08a_new\project.abs", "replaceMode": "whole", "content": "# ============================================
# Blockly ABS File - Clawd Pixel Crab Monitor
# ESP32-C3 + 0.96" TFT(ST7735) - Clawd Style Animations
# ============================================

arduino_global()
    custom_code("#define TFT_CS 10\n#define TFT_DC 9\n#define TFT_RST 8\n#define TFT_BLK 5\n#define PIN_BUZZER 4\n#define PIN_SOLENOID 3\n#define PIN_BUTTON 2")
    variable_define_scoped(global, "gState", int, math_number(0))
    variable_define_scoped(global, "gLastState", int, math_number(-1))
    variable_define_scoped(global, "gAnimFrame", int, math_number(0))
    variable_define_scoped(global, "gMuted", int, math_number(0))
    variable_define_scoped(global, "gBtnFlag", int, math_number(0))
    variable_define_scoped(global, "gSolenoidEnd", unsigned long, math_number(0))

arduino_setup()
    serial_begin(Serial, 115200)
    tft_init(ST7735, math_number(10), math_number(9), math_number(7), math_number(6), math_number(8), math_number(5), math_number(160), math_number(80))
    tft_set_rotation("1")
    tft_fill_screen(tft_color565(math_number(0), math_number(0), math_number(0)))
    tft_set_text_size(math_number(1))
    tft_print(math_number(55), math_number(30), text("Clawd"))
    tft_print(math_number(45), math_number(45), text("Monitor"))
    time_delay(math_number(1500))
    io_pinmode(math_number(3), io_mode(OUTPUT))
    io_digitalwrite(math_number(3), io_state(LOW))
    io_pinmode(math_number(4), io_mode(OUTPUT))
    io_pinmode(math_number(2), io_mode(INPUT_PULLUP))
    custom_code("attachInterrupt(digitalPinToInterrupt(2), []{ gBtnFlag = 1; }, FALLING);")

arduino_loop()
    controls_if(logic_compare(serial_available(Serial), GTE, math_number(1)))
        custom_code("String s = Serial.readStringUntil(0x0A); s.trim();\nif(s == \"idle\")      gState = 0;\nelse if(s == \"thinking\") gState = 1;\nelse if(s == \"coding\")   gState = 2;\nelse if(s == \"done\")     gState = 3;\nelse if(s == \"permission\") gState = 4;")
    controls_if(logic_compare(variables_get($gBtnFlag), EQ, math_number(1)))
        custom_code("gBtnFlag = 0;\ndelay(40);\nif(digitalRead(2) == LOW) {\n  unsigned long start = millis();\n  while(digitalRead(2) == LOW && millis() - start < 1200) { delay(20); }\n  unsigned long held = millis() - start;\n  if(held > 700) {\n    gMuted = !gMuted;\n    if(gMuted) { Serial.println(\"muted\"); }\n    else { tone(4, 800, 100); Serial.println(\"unmuted\"); }\n  } else {\n    Serial.println(\"btn\");\n    tone(4, 1000, 50);\n  }\n}")
    controls_if(logic_compare(variables_get($gState), NEQ, variables_get($gLastState)))
        variables_set($gLastState, variables_get($gState))
        variables_set($gAnimFrame, math_number(0))
        tft_fill_screen(tft_color565(math_number(0), math_number(0), math_number(0)))
        custom_code("if(gState == 3) { gSolenoidEnd = millis() + 300; digitalWrite(3, HIGH); }\nif(gState == 4) { gSolenoidEnd = millis() + 200; digitalWrite(3, HIGH); }")
    controls_if(logic_compare(variables_get($gState), EQ, math_number(0)))
        custom_code("static unsigned long t = 0;\nif(millis() - t > 400) {\n  t = millis(); gAnimFrame = (gAnimFrame + 1) % 10;\n  tft.fillScreen(ST77XX_BLACK);\n  int cx = 70, cy = 40;\n  uint16_t shell = tft.color565(255, 100, 50);\n  uint16_t light = tft.color565(255, 150, 80);\n  uint16_t dark = tft.color565(200, 80, 40);\n  tft.fillRect(cx - 8, cy - 6, 16, 12, shell);\n  tft.fillRect(cx - 6, cy - 4, 12, 8, light);\n  tft.fillRect(cx - 4, cy - 2, 8, 4, shell);\n  int eyeOff = (gAnimFrame % 4 == 0) ? 1 : 0;\n  tft.fillRect(cx - 5, cy - 5 - eyeOff, 3, 3, ST77XX_WHITE);\n  tft.fillRect(cx + 2, cy - 5 - eyeOff, 3, 3, ST77XX_WHITE);\n  if(gAnimFrame % 4 != 0) {\n    tft.fillRect(cx - 4, cy - 4, 2, 2, ST77XX_BLACK);\n    tft.fillRect(cx + 3, cy - 4, 2, 2, ST77XX_BLACK);\n  }\n  int clawY = cy + 2 + (gAnimFrame % 2);\n  tft.fillRect(cx - 12, clawY, 4, 3, shell);\n  tft.fillRect(cx - 10, clawY + 1, 2, 2, dark);\n  tft.fillRect(cx + 8, clawY, 4, 3, shell);\n  tft.fillRect(cx + 10, clawY + 1, 2, 2, dark);\n  for(int i = 0; i < 4; i++) {\n    int legX = cx - 6 + i * 4;\n    int legY = cy + 6 + (gAnimFrame % 2 == 0 ? 0 : 1);\n    tft.fillRect(legX, legY, 2, 3, shell);\n  }\n  if(gAnimFrame % 5 == 0) {\n    tft.drawCircle(cx + 15, cy - 8, 4, ST77XX_CYAN);\n    tft.drawCircle(cx + 15, cy - 8, 2, ST77XX_CYAN);\n  }\n  tft.setTextSize(1); tft.setTextColor(ST77XX_CYAN); tft.setCursor(5, 65); tft.print(\"idle...\");\n  if(!gMuted && gAnimFrame % 5 == 0) tone(4, 350, 30);\n}")
    controls_if(logic_compare(variables_get($gState), EQ, math_number(1)))
        custom_code("static unsigned long t = 0;\nif(millis() - t > 350) {\n  t = millis(); gAnimFrame = (gAnimFrame + 1) % 12;\n  tft.fillScreen(ST77XX_BLACK);\n  int cx = 70, cy = 40;\n  uint16_t shell = tft.color565(255, 100, 50);\n  uint16_t light = tft.color565(255, 150, 80);\n  uint16_t dark = tft.color565(200, 80, 40);\n  tft.fillRect(cx - 8, cy - 6, 16, 12, shell);\n  tft.fillRect(cx - 6, cy - 4, 12, 8, light);\n  tft.fillRect(cx - 4, cy - 2, 8, 4, shell);\n  tft.fillRect(cx - 5, cy - 5, 3, 3, ST77XX_WHITE);\n  tft.fillRect(cx + 2, cy - 5, 3, 3, ST77XX_WHITE);\n  tft.fillRect(cx - 4, cy - 4, 2, 2, ST77XX_BLACK);\n  tft.fillRect(cx + 3, cy - 4, 2, 2, ST77XX_BLACK);\n  int clawY = cy + 2;\n  tft.fillRect(cx - 12, clawY, 4, 3, shell);\n  tft.fillRect(cx - 10, clawY + 1, 2, 2, dark);\n  tft.fillRect(cx + 8, clawY, 4, 3, shell);\n  tft.fillRect(cx + 10, clawY + 1, 2, 2, dark);\n  for(int i = 0; i < 4; i++) {\n    int legX = cx - 6 + i * 4;\n    tft.fillRect(legX, cy + 6, 2, 3, shell);\n  }\n  int bubR = 6 + (gAnimFrame % 3) * 3;\n  tft.drawCircle(cx + 20, cy - 10, bubR, ST77XX_YELLOW);\n  tft.drawCircle(cx + 20, cy - 10, bubR - 3, ST77XX_YELLOW);\n  int dots = gAnimFrame % 4;\n  tft.setTextSize(1); tft.setTextColor(ST77XX_YELLOW);\n  tft.setCursor(cx + 18, cy - 15);\n  for(int d = 0; d <= dots; d++) tft.print(\".\");\n  tft.setTextSize(1); tft.setTextColor(ST77XX_YELLOW); tft.setCursor(5, 65); tft.print(\"thinking...\");\n  if(!gMuted) tone(4, 500 + gAnimFrame * 50, 25);\n}")
    controls_if(logic_compare(variables_get($gState), EQ, math_number(2)))
        custom_code("static unsigned long t = 0;\nif(millis() - t > 200) {\n  t = millis(); gAnimFrame = (gAnimFrame + 1) % 16;\n  tft.fillScreen(ST77XX_BLACK);\n  int cx = 70, cy = 40;\n  uint16_t shell = tft.color565(255, 100, 50);\n  uint16_t light = tft.color565(255, 150, 80);\n  uint16_t dark = tft.color565(200, 80, 40);\n  tft.fillRect(cx - 8, cy - 6, 16, 12, shell);\n  tft.fillRect(cx - 6, cy - 4, 12, 8, light);\n  tft.fillRect(cx - 4, cy - 2, 8, 4, shell);\n  tft.fillRect(cx - 5, cy - 5, 3, 3, ST77XX_WHITE);\n  tft.fillRect(cx + 2, cy - 5, 3, 3, ST77XX_WHITE);\n  tft.fillRect(cx - 4, cy - 4, 2, 2, ST77XX_BLACK);\n  tft.fillRect(cx + 3, cy - 4, 2, 2, ST77XX_BLACK);\n  int clawMove = (gAnimFrame % 2 == 0) ? -2 : 2;\n  tft.fillRect(cx - 12 + clawMove, cy + 2, 5, 4, shell);\n  tft.fillRect(cx - 9 + clawMove, cy + 3, 2, 2, dark);\n  tft.fillRect(cx + 7 - clawMove, cy + 2, 5, 4, shell);\n  tft.fillRect(cx + 10 - clawMove, cy + 3, 2, 2, dark);\n  for(int i = 0; i < 4; i++) {\n    int legX = cx - 6 + i * 4;\n    int legY = cy + 6 + ((i + gAnimFrame) % 2);\n    tft.fillRect(legX, legY, 2, 3, shell);\n  }\n  int bx = 10, by = 5;\n  tft.drawRect(bx, by, 50, 35, tft.color565(0, 100, 0));\n  tft.fillRect(bx + 1, by + 1, 48, 33, ST77XX_BLACK);\n  tft.setTextSize(1); tft.setTextColor(ST77XX_GREEN);\n  const char* lines[] = {\"> edit\", \"  save\", \"> build\", \"  test\", \"> run\", \"  done\"};\n  for(int i = 0; i < 4; i++) {\n    int li = (gAnimFrame / 2 + i) % 6;\n    tft.setCursor(bx + 3, by + 3 + i * 8);\n    tft.print(lines[li]);\n  }\n  tft.setTextSize(1); tft.setTextColor(ST77XX_GREEN); tft.setCursor(5, 65); tft.print(\"typing...\");\n  if(!gMuted && gAnimFrame % 4 == 0) tone(4, 800, 20);\n}")
    controls_if(logic_compare(variables_get($gState), EQ, math_number(3)))
        custom_code("static unsigned long t = 0;\nif(millis() - t > 250) {\n  t = millis(); gAnimFrame = (gAnimFrame + 1) % 12;\n  tft.fillScreen(ST77XX_BLACK);\n  int cx = 70, cy = 40;\n  int jumpOff = (gAnimFrame < 4) ? -((4 - gAnimFrame) * 3) : 0;\n  cy += jumpOff;\n  uint16_t shell = tft.color565(255, 100, 50);\n  uint16_t light = tft.color565(255, 150, 80);\n  uint16_t dark = tft.color565(200, 80, 40);\n  tft.fillRect(cx - 8, cy - 6, 16, 12, shell);\n  tft.fillRect(cx - 6, cy - 4, 12, 8, light);\n  tft.fillRect(cx - 4, cy - 2, 8, 4, shell);\n  int eyeH = (gAnimFrame < 4) ? 4 : 3;\n  tft.fillRect(cx - 5, cy - 5, 3, eyeH, ST77XX_WHITE);\n  tft.fillRect(cx + 2, cy - 5, 3, eyeH, ST77XX_WHITE);\n  tft.fillRect(cx - 4, cy - 4, 2, 2, ST77XX_BLACK);\n  tft.fillRect(cx + 3, cy - 4, 2, 2, ST77XX_BLACK);\n  int clawUp = (gAnimFrame < 4) ? -3 : 0;\n  tft.fillRect(cx - 12, cy + 2 + clawUp, 5, 4, shell);\n  tft.fillRect(cx - 9, cy + 3 + clawUp, 2, 2, dark);\n  tft.fillRect(cx + 7, cy + 2 + clawUp, 5, 4, shell);\n  tft.fillRect(cx + 10, cy + 3 + clawUp, 2, 2, dark);\n  for(int i = 0; i < 4; i++) {\n    int legX = cx - 6 + i * 4;\n    int legY = cy + 6 + ((gAnimFrame < 4) ? -2 : 0);\n    tft.fillRect(legX, legY, 2, 3, shell);\n  }\n  for(int i = 0; i < 8; i++) {\n    int sx = 10 + (i * 18 + gAnimFrame * 5) % 140;\n    int sy = 5 + (i * 10 + gAnimFrame * 3) % 70;\n    uint16_t colors[] = {ST77XX_RED, ST77XX_YELLOW, ST77XX_GREEN, ST77XX_CYAN, ST77XX_MAGENTA};\n    tft.fillRect(sx, sy, 3, 3, colors[i % 5]);\n  }\n  tft.setTextSize(2); tft.setTextColor(ST77XX_GREEN); tft.setCursor(100, 25); tft.print(\"OK!\");\n  tft.setTextSize(1); tft.setTextColor(ST77XX_GREEN); tft.setCursor(5, 65); tft.print(\"done! ^_^\");\n  if(!gMuted) tone(4, 1000 + gAnimFrame * 80, 50);\n}")
    controls_if(logic_compare(variables_get($gState), EQ, math_number(4)))
        custom_code("static unsigned long t = 0;\nif(millis() - t > 400) {\n  t = millis(); gAnimFrame = !gAnimFrame;\n  tft.fillScreen(gAnimFrame ? tft.color565(60, 0, 0) : ST77XX_BLACK);\n  int cx = 70, cy = 40;\n  uint16_t shell = tft.color565(255, 100, 50);\n  uint16_t light = tft.color565(255, 150, 80);\n  uint16_t dark = tft.color565(200, 80, 40);\n  tft.fillRect(cx - 8, cy - 6, 16, 12, shell);\n  tft.fillRect(cx - 6, cy - 4, 12, 8, light);\n  tft.fillRect(cx - 4, cy - 2, 8, 4, shell);\n  tft.fillRect(cx - 5, cy - 5, 4, 4, ST77XX_WHITE);\n  tft.fillRect(cx + 1, cy - 5, 4, 4, ST77XX_WHITE);\n  tft.fillRect(cx - 4, cy - 4, 2, 2, ST77XX_BLACK);\n  tft.fillRect(cx + 2, cy - 4, 2, 2, ST77XX_BLACK);\n  tft.fillRect(cx - 12, cy + 2, 5, 4, shell);\n  tft.fillRect(cx - 9, cy + 3, 2, 2, dark);\n  tft.fillRect(cx + 7, cy + 2, 5, 4, shell);\n  tft.fillRect(cx + 10, cy + 3, 2, 2, dark);\n  for(int i = 0; i < 4; i++) {\n    int legX = cx - 6 + i * 4;\n    tft.fillRect(legX, cy + 6, 2, 3, shell);\n  }\n  tft.drawRect(cx + 15, cy - 15, 20, 15, ST77XX_YELLOW);\n  tft.fillRect(cx + 16, cy - 14, 18, 13, ST77XX_BLACK);\n  tft.setTextSize(2); tft.setTextColor(ST77XX_YELLOW); tft.setCursor(cx + 20, cy - 12); tft.print(\"?\");\n  tft.setTextSize(1); tft.setTextColor(gAnimFrame ? ST77XX_RED : ST77XX_YELLOW);\n  tft.setCursor(5, 65); tft.print(\"[PRESS BTN]\");\n  if(!gMuted) tone(4, gAnimFrame ? 1500 : 500, 80);\n  gSolenoidEnd = millis() + 150;\n  digitalWrite(3, gAnimFrame);\n}")
    controls_if(logic_compare(time_millis(), GTE, variables_get($gSolenoidEnd)))

{"content":"#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Clawd Pixel Crab Monitor - Hardware Simulator
================================================
Preview the Clawd pixel crab animations without physical hardware.
Matches the ESP32 firmware animations pixel-for-pixel (scaled 2x).
Based on: https://github.com/rullerzhou-afk/clawd-on-desk

Run with: python simulator.py
Or via bridge: python bridge.py --mode simulate
"""

import tkinter as tk
from tkinter import ttk
import threading
import time
import math
import sys

try:
    import winsound
    HAS_WINSOUND = True
except ImportError:
    HAS_WINSOUND = False

# Clawd crab color palette (matching tft.color565 values)
SHELL_COLOR = '#FF6432'    # tft.color565(255, 100, 50) - orange-red shell
LIGHT_COLOR = '#FF9650'    # tft.color565(255, 150, 80) - light belly
DARK_COLOR  = '#C85028'    # tft.color565(200, 80, 40)  - dark accents

# ST77XX color equivalents
BLACK   = '#000000'
WHITE   = '#FFFFFF'
RED     = '#FF0000'
GREEN   = '#00FF00'
CYAN    = '#00FFFF'
YELLOW  = '#FFFF00'
MAGENTA = '#FF00FF'

SCALE = 2

class HardwareSimulator:
    def __init__(self):
        self.root = tk.Tk()
        self.root.title("🦀 Clawd Pixel Crab Monitor - Hardware Simulator")
        self.root.configure(bg='#1a1a1a')
        self.root.resizable(False, False)

        self.current_state = "idle"
        self.anim_frame = 0
        self.muted = False
        self.solenoid_active = False
        self.anim_running = True
        self.button_press_time = 0
        self.demo_active = False

        self._create_gui()
        self.anim_thread = threading.Thread(target=self._animation_loop, daemon=True)
        self.anim_thread.start()

    # ─── Drawing primitives ───

    def _rect(self, x, y, w, h, color):
        sx, sy = x * SCALE, y * SCALE
        self.canvas.create_rectangle(sx, sy, sx + w * SCALE, sy + h * SCALE, fill=color, outline='')

    def _draw_rect(self, x, y, w, h, color):
        sx, sy = x * SCALE, y * SCALE
        self.canvas.create_rectangle(sx, sy, sx + w * SCALE, sy + h * SCALE, outline=color, width=SCALE)

    def _circle(self, cx, cy, r, color):
        sx, sy = cx * SCALE, cy * SCALE
        sr = r * SCALE
        self.canvas.create_oval(sx - sr, sy - sr, sx + sr, sy + sr, outline=color, width=SCALE)

    def _text(self, x, y, txt, color, size=1):
        sx, sy = x * SCALE, y * SCALE
        fs = max(8, size * 10 * SCALE)
        font = ('Courier', fs, 'bold') if size >= 2 else ('Courier', fs)
        self.canvas.create_text(sx, sy, text=txt, fill=color, font=font, anchor='nw')

    def _clear(self, color=BLACK):
        self.canvas.create_rectangle(0, 0, 160 * SCALE, 80 * SCALE, fill=color, outline='')

    # ─── Clawd Crab Drawing ───

    def _draw_crab(self, cx, cy, eyes='normal', claw_anim=0, leg_anim=0, jump=0):
        """
        Draw Clawd pixel crab at (cx, cy).
        eyes: 'normal', 'blink', 'wide', 'sad'
        claw_anim: -2..2 claw offset
        leg_anim: 0..1 leg offset
        jump: vertical offset for done state
        """
        cy += jump
        shell = SHELL_COLOR
        light = LIGHT_COLOR
        dark  = DARK_COLOR

        # Shell body (16x12)
        self._rect(cx - 8, cy - 6, 16, 12, shell)
        # Belly highlight (12x8)
        self._rect(cx - 6, cy - 4, 12, 8, light)
        # Center shell line (8x4)
        self._rect(cx - 4, cy - 2, 8, 4, shell)

        # Eyes
        ey = cy - 5
        if eyes == 'blink':
            # Closed eyes - horizontal line
            self._rect(cx - 5, ey + 1, 3, 1, WHITE)
            self._rect(cx + 2, ey + 1, 3, 1, WHITE)
        elif eyes == 'wide':
            # Big eyes for permission
            self._rect(cx - 5, ey, 4, 4, WHITE)
            self._rect(cx + 1, ey, 4, 4, WHITE)
            self._rect(cx - 4, ey + 1, 2, 2, BLACK)
            self._rect(cx + 2, ey + 1, 2, 2, BLACK)
        else:
            # Normal eyes
            self._rect(cx - 5, ey, 3, 3, WHITE)
            self._rect(cx + 2, ey, 3, 3, WHITE)
            self._rect(cx - 4, ey + 1, 2, 2, BLACK)
            self._rect(cx + 3, ey + 1, 2, 2, BLACK)

        # Claws (with animation)
        cl_y = cy + 2 + abs(claw_anim) // 2
        # Left claw
        self._rect(cx - 12 + claw_anim, cl_y, 4, 3, shell)
        self._rect(cx - 10 + claw_anim, cl_y + 1, 2, 2, dark)
        # Right claw
        self._rect(cx + 8 - claw_anim, cl_y, 4, 3, shell)
        self._rect(cx + 10 - claw_anim, cl_y + 1, 2, 2, dark)

        # Legs (4 pairs)
        for i in range(4):
            lx = cx - 6 + i * 4
            ly = cy + 6 + (leg_anim if i % 2 == 0 else 0)
            self._rect(lx, ly, 2, 3, shell)

    # ─── State Animations (matching ESP32) ───

    def _draw_idle(self):
        f = self.anim_frame % 10
        self._clear()

        # Crab with occasional blink
        eyes = 'blink' if f % 4 == 0 else 'normal'
        leg_anim = 1 if f % 2 == 0 else 0
        self._draw_crab(70, 40, eyes=eyes, leg_anim=leg_anim)

        # Occasional bubble
        if f % 5 == 0:
            self._circle(85, 32, 4, CYAN)
            self._circle(85, 32, 2, CYAN)

        self._text(5, 65, "idle...", CYAN, 1)
        if f % 5 == 0 and not self.muted:
            self._beep(350, 30)

    def _draw_thinking(self):
        f = self.anim_frame % 12
        self._clear()

        self._draw_crab(70, 40, eyes='normal')

        # Thought bubble
        bub_r = 6 + (f % 3) * 3
        self._circle(90, 30, bub_r, YELLOW)
        self._circle(90, 30, bub_r - 3, YELLOW)

        # Animated dots
        dots = "." * ((f % 4) + 1)
        self._text(88, 25, dots, YELLOW, 1)

        self._text(5, 65, "thinking...", YELLOW, 1)
        if not self.muted:
            self._beep(500 + f * 50, 25)

    def _draw_coding(self):
        f = self.anim_frame % 16
        self._clear()

        # Crab with typing claws
        claw_off = -2 if f % 2 == 0 else 2
        leg_anim = (f) % 2
        self._draw_crab(70, 40, claw_anim=claw_off, leg_anim=leg_anim)

        # Mini terminal window
        bx, by = 10, 5
        self._draw_rect(bx, by, 50, 35, '#006400')
        self._rect(bx + 1, by + 1, 48, 33, BLACK)

        code_lines = ["> edit", "  save", "> build", "  test", "> run", "  done"]
        for i in range(4):
            li = (f // 2 + i) % 6
            self._text(bx + 3, by + 3 + i * 8, code_lines[li], GREEN, 1)

        self._text(5, 65, "typing...", GREEN, 1)
        if f % 4 == 0 and not self.muted:
            self._beep(800, 20)

    def _draw_done(self):
        f = self.anim_frame % 12
        self._clear()

        # Jump animation
        jump = -((4 - f) * 3) if f < 4 else 0
        eyes = 'wide' if f < 4 else 'normal'
        claw_up = -3 if f < 4 else 0
        leg_jump = -2 if f < 4 else 0

        self._draw_crab(70, 40, eyes=eyes, claw_anim=claw_up, leg_anim=0, jump=jump)

        # Confetti
        colors = [RED, YELLOW, GREEN, CYAN, MAGENTA]
        for i in range(8):
            sx = 10 + (i * 18 + f * 5) % 140
            sy = 5 + (i * 10 + f * 3) % 70
            self._rect(sx, sy, 3, 3, colors[i % 5])

        self._text(100, 25, "OK!", GREEN, 2)
        self._text(5, 65, "done! ^_^", GREEN, 1)
        if not self.muted:
            self._beep(1000 + f * 80, 50)
        if f == 0:
            self._solenoid(300)

    def _draw_permission(self):
        f = self.anim_frame % 2
        bg = '#3C0000' if f else BLACK
        self._clear(bg)

        self._draw_crab(70, 40, eyes='wide')

        # Question mark dialog
        qx, qy = 85, 25
        self._draw_rect(qx, qy, 20, 15, YELLOW)
        self._rect(qx + 1, qy + 1, 18, 13, BLACK)
        self._text(qx + 5, qy + 1, "?", YELLOW, 2)

        txt_color = RED if f else YELLOW
        self._text(5, 65, "[PRESS BTN]", txt_color, 1)

        if not self.muted:
            self._beep(1500 if f else 500, 80)
        if f:
            self._solenoid(150)

    # ─── Sound & Solenoid ───

    def _beep(self, freq, dur):
        if self.muted or not HAS_WINSOUND:
            return
        try:
            threading.Thread(target=lambda: winsound.Beep(freq, dur), daemon=True).start()
        except:
            pass
        self.buzzer.itemconfig('bz', fill='#00ff00')
        self.root.after(100, lambda: self.buzzer.itemconfig('bz', fill='gray'))

    def _solenoid(self, ms):
        if self.solenoid_active:
            return
        self.solenoid_active = True
        self.solenoid_cv.itemconfig('sol', fill='#ff6600')
        self.solenoid_lb.config(text="ON ⚡")
        self.root.after(ms, self._solenoid_off)

    def _solenoid_off(self):
        self.solenoid_active = False
        self.solenoid_cv.itemconfig('sol', fill='gray')
        self.solenoid_lb.config(text="OFF")

    # ─── Button ───

    def _btn_down(self, e):
        self.btn_cv.itemconfig('btn', fill='#00ff00')
        self.button_press_time = time.time()

    def _btn_up(self, e):
        self.btn_cv.itemconfig('btn', fill='#444')
        held = time.time() - self.button_press_time
        if held > 0.7:
            self.muted = not self.muted
            self.mute_var.set(self.muted)
            self.btn_log.config(text="🔇 Muted" if self.muted else "🔊 Unmuted")
            if not self.muted:
                self._beep(800, 100)
        else:
            self.btn_log.config(text="✓ Confirmed!")
            if not self.muted:
                self._beep(1000, 50)
            if self.current_state == "permission":
                self._set_state("idle")

    # ─── State Control ───

    def _set_state(self, state):
        self.current_state = state
        self.anim_frame = 0
        icons = {"idle": "💤 Idle", "thinking": "🤔 Thinking", "coding": "💻 Typing",
                 "done": "✅ Done", "permission": "⚠️ Permission"}
        self.status_var.set(icons.get(state, state))
        if state == "done":
            self._solenoid(300)
        elif state == "permission":
            self._solenoid(200)

    def _toggle_mute(self):
        self.muted = self.mute_var.get()
        self.btn_log.config(text="🔇 Muted" if self.muted else "🔊 Unmuted")

    # ─── Animation Loop ───

    def _animation_loop(self):
        delays = {"idle": 0.4, "thinking": 0.35, "coding": 0.2, "done": 0.25, "permission": 0.4}
        while self.anim_running:
            self.root.after(0, self._render)
            time.sleep(delays.get(self.current_state, 0.05))

    def _render(self):
        self.canvas.delete('all')
        draw = {"idle": self._draw_idle, "thinking": self._draw_thinking,
                "coding": self._draw_coding, "done": self._draw_done,
                "permission": self._draw_permission}
        fn = draw.get(self.current_state)
        if fn:
            fn()
        self.anim_frame += 1

    # ─── Demo Mode ───

    def _toggle_demo(self):
        self.demo_active = self.demo_var.get()
        if self.demo_active:
            self._demo_step(0)

    def _demo_step(self, idx):
        seq = [("idle", 3), ("thinking", 4), ("coding", 5), ("done", 3),
               ("idle", 2), ("permission", 4), ("coding", 4), ("done", 3)]
        if not self.demo_active or not self.anim_running:
            return
        state, dur = seq[idx % len(seq)]
        self._set_state(state)
        self.root.after(int(dur * 1000), lambda: self._demo_step(idx + 1))

    # ─── GUI ───

    def _create_gui(self):
        style = ttk.Style()
        style.configure('TFrame', background='#1a1a1a')
        style.configure('TLabel', background='#1a1a1a', foreground='white')
        style.configure('TLabelframe', background='#1a1a1a', foreground='white')
        style.configure('TLabelframe.Label', background='#1a1a1a', foreground='white')

        main = ttk.Frame(self.root, padding=8)
        main.pack(fill=tk.BOTH, expand=True)

        # Title
        ttk.Label(main, text="🦀 Clawd Pixel Crab — ESP32 Monitor Simulator",
                   font=('Arial', 11, 'bold'), foreground='#FF6432').pack(pady=(0, 5))

        # TFT Display
        tft_frame = ttk.LabelFrame(main, text="📺 TFT 160×80 (×2)", padding=3)
        tft_frame.pack()
        self.canvas = tk.Canvas(tft_frame, width=160 * SCALE, height=80 * SCALE,
                                bg='black', highlightthickness=2, highlightbackground='#444')
        self.canvas.pack()

        # Status
        sf = ttk.Frame(main)
        sf.pack(fill=tk.X, pady=4)
        ttk.Label(sf, text="State:", font=('Arial', 10)).pack(side=tk.LEFT)
        self.status_var = tk.StringVar(value="💤 Idle")
        ttk.Label(sf, textvariable=self.status_var, font=('Arial', 11, 'bold'),
                  foreground='#00ff88').pack(side=tk.LEFT, padx=8)

        # Hardware row
        hw = ttk.Frame(main)
        hw.pack(fill=tk.X, pady=3)

        # Buzzer
        bz_f = ttk.LabelFrame(hw, text="🔊", padding=2)
        bz_f.pack(side=tk.LEFT, padx=4)
        self.buzzer = tk.Canvas(bz_f, width=20, height=20, bg='#1a1a1a', highlightthickness=0)
        self.buzzer.pack()
        self.buzzer.create_oval(2, 2, 18, 18, fill='gray', outline='#666', tags='bz')

        # Solenoid
        sol_f = ttk.LabelFrame(hw, text="⚡", padding=2)
        sol_f.pack(side=tk.LEFT, padx=4)
        self.solenoid_cv = tk.Canvas(sol_f, width=40, height=18, bg='#1a1a1a', highlightthickness=0)
        self.solenoid_cv.pack()
        self.solenoid_cv.create_rectangle(2, 2, 38, 16, fill='gray', outline='#666', tags='sol')
        self.solenoid_lb = ttk.Label(sol_f, text="OFF", font=('Arial', 8))
        self.solenoid_lb.pack()

        # Mute
        self.mute_var = tk.BooleanVar(value=False)
        ttk.Checkbutton(hw, text="🔇 Mute", variable=self.mute_var,
                        command=self._toggle_mute).pack(side=tk.LEFT, padx=8)

        # Button
        btn_f = ttk.LabelFrame(hw, text="🔘 Button", padding=2)
        btn_f.pack(side=tk.LEFT, padx=4)
        self.btn_cv = tk.Canvas(btn_f, width=40, height=24, bg='#1a1a1a', highlightthickness=0)
        self.btn_cv.pack()
        self.btn_cv.create_rectangle(8, 4, 32, 20, fill='#444', outline='#888', tags='btn')
        self.btn_cv.bind('<Button-1>', self._btn_down)
        self.btn_cv.bind('<ButtonRelease-1>', self._btn_up)
        self.btn_log = ttk.Label(btn_f, text="click", font=('Arial', 7))
        self.btn_log.pack()

        # State buttons
        ctrl = ttk.LabelFrame(main, text="🎮 State Control", padding=4)
        ctrl.pack(fill=tk.X, pady=4)

        states = [("💤 Idle", "idle", '#00cccc'), ("🤔 Thinking", "thinking", '#cccc00'),
                  ("💻 Typing", "coding", '#00cc00'), ("✅ Done", "done", '#00cc00'),
                  ("⚠️ Permission", "permission", '#cc4444')]
        for txt, st, fg in states:
            tk.Button(ctrl, text=txt, command=lambda s=st: self._set_state(s),
                      bg='#2a2a2a', fg=fg, font=('Arial', 9), width=13,
                      activebackground='#444', activeforeground=fg, relief=tk.RAISED, bd=2
                     ).pack(side=tk.LEFT, padx=1)

        # Bottom
        bot = ttk.Frame(main)
        bot.pack(fill=tk.X, pady=6)
        self.demo_var = tk.BooleanVar(value=False)
        ttk.Checkbutton(bot, text="🔄 Auto Demo", variable=self.demo_var,
                        command=self._toggle_demo).pack(side=tk.LEFT)
        ttk.Button(bot, text="❌ Quit", command=self._quit).pack(side=tk.RIGHT)

        ttk.Label(main, text="Clawd pixel crab — matches ESP32 firmware pixel-for-pixel",
                  font=('Arial', 8), foreground='#666').pack()

    def _quit(self):
        self.anim_running = False
        self.root.quit()
        self.root.destroy()

    def run(self):
        self.root.update_idletasks()
        w, h = self.root.winfo_width(), self.root.winfo_height()
        x = (self.root.winfo_screenwidth() - w) // 2
        y = (self.root.winfo_screenheight() - h) // 2
        self.root.geometry(f'+{x}+{y}')
        self.root.mainloop()

def run_simulator():
    HardwareSimulator().run()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    run_simulator()

int gLastState = (-1);
int gAnimFrame = 0;
int gMuted = 0;
int gBtnFlag = 0;
unsigned long gSolenoidEnd = 0;

  tft.setTextSize(1);
  tft.setCursor(55 , 30 );
  tft.print("Clawd");
  tft.setCursor(45 , 45 );
  tft.print("Monitor");
  delay(1500);
  pinMode(3, OUTPUT);
  digitalWrite(3, LOW);
  pinMode(4, OUTPUT);
  pinMode(2, INPUT_PULLUP);
  attachInterrupt(digitalPinToInterrupt(2), []{ gBtnFlag = 1; }, FALLING);

}

void loop() {

  if (Serial.available() >= 1) {
    String s = Serial.readStringUntil(0x0A); s.trim();
    if(s == "idle")      gState = 0;
    else if(s == "thinking") gState = 1;
    else if(s == "coding")   gState = 2;
    else if(s == "done")     gState = 3;
    else if(s == "permission") gState = 4;
  }
  if (gBtnFlag == 1) {
    gBtnFlag = 0;
    delay(40);
    if(digitalRead(2) == LOW) {
      unsigned long start = millis();
      while(digitalRead(2) == LOW && millis() - start < 1200) { delay(20); }
      unsigned long held = millis() - start;
      if(held > 700) {
        gMuted = !gMuted;
        if(gMuted) { Serial.println("muted"); }
        else { tone(4, 800, 100); Serial.println("unmuted"); }
      } else {
        Serial.println("btn");
        tone(4, 1000, 50);
      }
    }
  }
  if (gState != gLastState) {
    gLastState = gState;
    gAnimFrame = 0;
    tft.fillScreen(tft.color565(0, 0, 0));
    if(gState == 3) { gSolenoidEnd = millis() + 300; digitalWrite(3, HIGH); }
    if(gState == 4) { gSolenoidEnd = millis() + 200; digitalWrite(3, HIGH); }

  if (gState == 0) {
    static unsigned long t = 0;

      t = millis(); gAnimFrame = (gAnimFrame + 1) % 10;
      tft.fillScreen(ST77XX_BLACK);
      int cx = 70, cy = 40;
      uint16_t shell = tft.color565(255, 100, 50);
      uint16_t light = tft.color565(255, 150, 80);
      uint16_t dark = tft.color565(200, 80, 40);
      tft.fillRect(cx - 8, cy - 6, 16, 12, shell);
      tft.fillRect(cx - 6, cy - 4, 12, 8, light);
      tft.fillRect(cx - 4, cy - 2, 8, 4, shell);
      int eyeOff = (gAnimFrame % 4 == 0) ? 1 : 0;
      tft.fillRect(cx - 5, cy - 5 - eyeOff, 3, 3, ST77XX_WHITE);
      tft.fillRect(cx + 2, cy - 5 - eyeOff, 3, 3, ST77XX_WHITE);
      if(gAnimFrame % 4 != 0) {
        tft.fillRect(cx - 4, cy - 4, 2, 2, ST77XX_BLACK);
        tft.fillRect(cx + 3, cy - 4, 2, 2, ST77XX_BLACK);
      }
      int clawY = cy + 2 + (gAnimFrame % 2);
      tft.fillRect(cx - 12, clawY, 4, 3, shell);

      }

      if(!gMuted && gAnimFrame % 5 == 0) tone(4, 350, 30);

      tft.fillRect(cx + 8, clawY, 4, 3, shell);

    static unsigned long t = 0;
    if(millis() - t > 250) {
      t = millis(); gAnimFrame = (gAnimFrame + 1) % 12;
      tft.fillScreen(ST77XX_BLACK);
      int cx = 70, cy = 40;
      int jumpOff = (gAnimFrame < 4) ? -((4 - gAnimFrame) * 3) : 0;
      cy += jumpOff;
      uint16_t shell = tft.color565(255, 100, 50);
      uint16_t light = tft.color565(255, 150, 80);
      uint16_t dark = tft.color565(200, 80, 40);
      tft.fillRect(cx - 8, cy - 6, 16, 12, shell);
      tft.fillRect(cx - 6, cy - 4, 12, 8, light);
      tft.fillRect(cx - 4, cy - 2, 8, 4, shell);
      int eyeH = (gAnimFrame < 4) ? 4 : 3;
      tft.fillRect(cx - 5, cy - 5, 3, eyeH, ST77XX_WHITE);
      tft.fillRect(cx + 2, cy - 5, 3, eyeH, ST77XX_WHITE);
      tft.fillRect(cx - 4, cy - 4, 2, 2, ST77XX_BLACK);
      tft.fillRect(cx + 3, cy - 4, 2, 2, ST77XX_BLACK);
      int clawUp = (gAnimFrame < 4) ? -3 : 0;
      tft.fillRect(cx - 12, cy + 2 + clawUp, 5, 4, shell);
      tft.fillRect(cx - 9, cy + 3 + clawUp, 2, 2, dark);
      tft.fillRect(cx + 7, cy + 2 + clawUp, 5, 4, shell);
      tft.fillRect(cx + 10, cy + 3 + clawUp, 2, 2, dark);
      for(int i = 0; i < 4; i++) {
        int legX = cx - 6 + i * 4;
        int legY = cy + 6 + ((gAnimFrame < 4) ? -2 : 0);
        tft.fillRect(legX, legY, 2, 3, shell);
      }
      for(int i = 0; i < 8; i++) {
        int sx = 10 + (i * 18 + gAnimFrame * 5) % 140;
        int sy = 5 + (i * 10 + gAnimFrame * 3) % 70;
        uint16_t colors[] = {ST77XX_RED, ST77XX_YELLOW, ST77XX_GREEN, ST77XX_CYAN, ST77XX_MAGENTA};
        tft.fillRect(sx, sy, 3, 3, colors[i % 5]);
      }
      tft.setTextSize(2); tft.setTextColor(ST77XX_GREEN); tft.setCursor(100, 25); tft.print("OK!");
      tft.setTextSize(1); tft.setTextColor(ST77XX_GREEN); tft.setCursor(5, 65); tft.print("done! ^_^");
      if(!gMuted) tone(4, 1000 + gAnimFrame * 80, 50);
    }
  }
  if (gState == 4) {
    static unsigned long t = 0;
    if(millis() - t > 400) {
      t = millis(); gAnimFrame = !gAnimFrame;
      tft.fillScreen(gAnimFrame ? tft.color565(60, 0, 0) : ST77XX_BLACK);
      int cx = 70, cy = 40;
      uint16_t shell = tft.color565(255, 100, 50);
      uint16_t light = tft.color565(255, 150, 80);
      uint16_t dark = tft.color565(200, 80, 40);
      tft.fillRect(cx - 8, cy - 6, 16, 12, shell);
      tft.fillRect(cx - 6, cy - 4, 12, 8, light);
      tft.fillRect(cx - 4, cy - 2, 8, 4, shell);
      tft.fillRect(cx - 5, cy - 5, 4, 4, ST77XX_WHITE);
      tft.fillRect(cx + 1, cy - 5, 4, 4, ST77XX_WHITE);
      tft.fillRect(cx - 4, cy - 4, 2, 2, ST77XX_BLACK);
      tft.fillRect(cx + 2, cy - 4, 2, 2, ST77XX_BLACK);
      tft.fillRect(cx - 12, cy + 2, 5, 4, shell);
      tft.fillRect(cx - 9, cy + 3, 2, 2, dark);
      tft.fillRect(cx + 7, cy + 2, 5, 4, shell);
      tft.fillRect(cx + 10, cy + 3, 2, 2, dark);
      for(int i = 0; i < 4; i++) {
        int legX = cx - 6 + i * 4;
        tft.fillRect(legX, cy + 6, 2, 3, shell);
      }
      tft.drawRect(cx + 15, cy - 15, 20, 15, ST77XX_YELLOW);
      tft.fillRect(cx + 16, cy - 14, 18, 13, ST77XX_BLACK);
      tft.setTextSize(2); tft.setTextColor(ST77XX_YELLOW); tft.setCursor(cx + 20, cy - 12); tft.print("?");
      tft.setTextSize(1); tft.setTextColor(gAnimFrame ? ST77XX_RED : ST77XX_YELLOW);
      tft.setCursor(5, 65); tft.print("[PRESS BTN]");
      if(!gMuted) tone(4, gAnimFrame ? 1500 : 500, 80);
      gSolenoidEnd = millis() + 150;
      digitalWrite(3, gAnimFrame);

  }
  if (millis() >= gSolenoidEnd) {
    digitalWrite(3, LOW);
  }
  delay(30);

}"

"""Download all Clawd SVG animation files from GitHub"""
import os
import requests
import base64

# SVG files from theme.json states
SVG_FILES = [
    "clawd-idle-follow.svg",
    "clawd-idle-yawn.svg",
    "clawd-idle-doze.svg",
    "clawd-collapse-sleep.svg",
    "clawd-working-thinking.svg",
    "clawd-working-typing.svg",
    "clawd-headphones-groove.svg",
    "clawd-working-sweeping.svg",
    "clawd-error.svg",
    "clawd-happy.svg",
    "clawd-notification.svg",
    "clawd-working-carrying.svg",
    "clawd-sleeping.svg",
    "clawd-wake.svg",
    "clawd-working-building.svg",
    "clawd-working-juggling.svg",
    "clawd-idle-look.svg",
    "clawd-idle-bubble.svg",
    "clawd-idle-reading.svg",
    "clawd-working-debugger.svg",
    "clawd-react-drag.svg",
    "clawd-react-left.svg",
    "clawd-react-right.svg",
    "clawd-react-annoyed.svg",
    "clawd-react-double.svg",
    "clawd-react-double-jump.svg",
    "clawd-mini-idle.svg",
    "clawd-mini-alert.svg",
    "clawd-mini-happy.svg",
    "clawd-mini-enter.svg",
    "clawd-mini-peek.svg",
    "clawd-mini-typing.svg",
    "clawd-mini-crabwalk.svg",
    "clawd-mini-enter-sleep.svg",
    "clawd-mini-sleep.svg",
]

BASE_URL = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rullerzhou-afk/clawd-on-desk/main/themes/clawd/"
OUTPUT_DIR = "E:/硬件开发/clawd_svgs"

def download_svgs():
    os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)
    
    for svg_file in SVG_FILES:
        url = BASE_URL + svg_file
        try:
            resp = requests.get(url, timeout=10)
            if resp.status_code == 200:
                path = os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, svg_file)
                with open(path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                    f.write(resp.text)
                print(f"✓ {svg_file}")
            else:
                print(f"✗ {svg_file} (status {resp.status_code})")
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"✗ {svg_file} - {e}")
    
    print(f"\nDownloaded to: {OUTPUT_DIR}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    download_svgs()"}

我需要：

2. 解析 SVG 中的像素坐标和颜色
3. 将这些像素数据转换为 ESP32 的 tft.fillRect() 调用

让我先下载几个核心 SVG 文件看看它们的结构。</think>系统没有 git，我改用 Python 直接下载 SVG 文件内容并解析像素数据。

toolCalls.arguments:
{"path": "E:\硬件开发\project_jun08a_new\download_clawd_svgs.py", "replaceMode": "whole", "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Download Clawd SVG files as text and extract pixel data"""
import os
import requests
import re

BASE_URL = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rullerzhou-afk/clawd-on-desk/main/themes/clawd/"
OUTPUT_DIR = "E:/硬件开发/clawd_svgs"

# Core animation files from theme.json
SVG_FILES = [
    "clawd-idle-follow.svg",
    "clawd-idle-yawn.svg",
    "clawd-idle-doze.svg",
    "clawd-collapse-sleep.svg",
    "clawd-working-thinking.svg",
    "clawd-working-typing.svg",
    "clawd-headphones-groove.svg",
    "clawd-working-sweeping.svg",
    "clawd-error.svg",
    "clawd-happy.svg",
    "clawd-notification.svg",
    "clawd-working-carrying.svg",
    "clawd-sleeping.svg",
    "clawd-wake.svg",
    "clawd-working-building.svg",
    "clawd-working-juggling.svg",
    "clawd-idle-look.svg",
    "clawd-idle-bubble.svg",
    "clawd-idle-reading.svg",
    "clawd-working-debugger.svg",
    "clawd-react-drag.svg",
    "clawd-react-left.svg",
    "clawd-react-right.svg",
    "clawd-react-annoyed.svg",
    "clawd-react-double.svg",
    "clawd-react-double-jump.svg",
]

def download_svgs():
    os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)
    
    for svg_file in SVG_FILES:
        url = BASE_URL + svg_file
        try:
            resp = requests.get(url, timeout=10)
            if resp.status_code == 200:
                path = os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, svg_file)
                with open(path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                    f.write(resp.text)
                print(f"OK {svg_file} ({len(resp.text)} bytes)")
            else:
                print(f"FAIL {svg_file} (status {resp.status_code})")
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"FAIL {svg_file} - {e}")
    
    print(f"\nDownloaded to: {OUTPUT_DIR}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    download_svgs()"}

{"path": "E:\硬件开发\project_jun08a_new\download_clawd_svgs.py", "replaceMode": "whole", "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Download Clawd SVG files from assets/svg directory"""
import os
import requests

BASE_URL = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rullerzhou-afk/clawd-on-desk/main/assets/svg/"
OUTPUT_DIR = "E:/硬件开发/clawd_svgs"

# Core animation files
SVG_FILES = [
    "clawd-idle-follow.svg",      # 基础idle状态
    "clawd-idle-doze.svg",        # 打瞌睡
    "clawd-idle-yawn.svg",        # 打哈欠
    "clawd-idle-look.svg",        # 看左右
    "clawd-idle-bubble.svg",      # 冒泡思考
    "clawd-idle-reading.svg",     # 阅读
    "clawd-working-typing.svg",   # 打字工作
    "clawd-working-thinking.svg", # 思考
    "clawd-working-building.svg", # 构建
    "clawd-working-juggling.svg", # 杂耍
    "clawd-headphones-groove.svg", # 戴耳机摇摆
    "clawd-happy.svg",            # 开心
    "clawd-error.svg",            # 错误
    "clawd-notification.svg",     # 通知
    "clawd-collapse-sleep.svg",   # 睡觉
    "clawd-sleeping.svg",         # 深睡
    "clawd-wake.svg",             # 醒来
]

def download_svgs():
    os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)
    
    for svg_file in SVG_FILES:
        url = BASE_URL + svg_file
        try:
            resp = requests.get(url, timeout=10)
            if resp.status_code == 200:
                path = os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, svg_file)
                with open(path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                    f.write(resp.text)
                print(f"OK {svg_file} ({len(resp.text)} bytes)")
            else:
                print(f"FAIL {svg_file} (status {resp.status_code})")
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"FAIL {svg_file} - {e}")
    
    print(f"\nDownloaded to: {OUTPUT_DIR}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    download_svgs()"}

<toolResult><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="-15 -25 45 45" width="500" height="500">
  <defs>
    <style>
      .breathe-anim {
        transform-origin: 7.5px 13px;
        animation: breathe 3.2s infinite ease-in-out;
      }

      .eyes-blink {
        transform-origin: 7.5px 9px;
        animation: eye-blink 4s infinite ease-in-out;
      }

      #eyes-js, #body-js, #shadow-js {
        transition: transform 0.2s ease-out;
      }
      #shadow-js {
        transform-origin: 7.5px 15px;
      }

      @keyframes breathe {
        0%, 100% { transform: scale(1, 1) translate(0, 0); }
        50% { transform: scale(1.02, 0.98) translate(0, 0.5px); }
      }

      @keyframes eye-blink {
        0%, 10%, 100% { transform: scaleY(1); }
        5% { transform: scaleY(0.1); }
      }
    </style>
  </defs>

  <!-- Ground Shadow (JS-controlled horizontal shift) -->
  <g id="shadow-js">
    <rect x="3" y="15" width="9" height="1" fill="#000000" opacity="0.5"/>
  </g>

  <!-- Static Legs (slightly taller for upward body shift) -->
  <g id="legs" fill="#DE886D">
    <rect x="3" y="11" width="1" height="4"/>
    <rect x="5" y="11" width="1" height="4"/>
    <rect x="9" y="11" width="1" height="4"/>
    <rect x="11" y="11" width="1" height="4"/>
  </g>

  <!-- Upper Body (JS body-shift + CSS breathe) -->
  <g id="body-js">
    <g class="breathe-anim">
      <!-- Torso -->
      <rect id="torso" x="2" y="6" width="11" height="7" fill="#DE886D"/>
      <!-- Left Arm -->
      <rect x="0" y="9" width="2" height="2" fill="#DE886D"/>
      <!-- Right Arm -->
      <rect x="13" y="9" width="2" height="2" fill="#DE886D"/>
      <!-- Eyes: JS translate wrapper + CSS blink -->
      <g id="eyes-js" fill="#000000">
        <g class="eyes-blink">
          <rect x="4" y="8" width="1" height="2"/>
          <rect x="10" y="8" width="1" height="2"/>
        </g>
      </g>
    </g>
  </g>
</svg>
